findings.finding: Phase 5 hook modification references $PHASE_NUM and $TOTAL_PHASES as bare variables, but these are never established from positional args in the hook snippet
findings.claim: The replacement code uses $PHASE_NUM and $TOTAL_PHASES which are undefined in the hook context, so the early-exit guard `[[ $PHASE_NUM -lt $TOTAL_PHASES ]] && exit 0` will always evaluate incorrectly.
findings.evidence: Plan snippet: `if [[ "$BR3_PHASE_LESSONS" == "on" ]]; then lessons-learned.sh "$@"; fi; [[ "$PHASE_NUM" -lt "$TOTAL_PHASES" ]] && exit 0` — $PHASE_NUM/$TOTAL_PHASES are not derived from $@ or any export in the described plan.

findings.finding: Phase 5 `lessons-learned.sh` described as piping `claude -p` output to `echo`, but echo does not read stdin
findings.claim: The described implementation `pipe output to echo "$(date...) ..." >> decisions.log` does not actually append the lesson text; echo ignores stdin, so decisions.log receives no lesson content.
findings.evidence: Plan: "pipe output to `echo "$(date -u +%FT%TZ) [lessons] build=$BUILD phase=$N ..." >> $PROJECT/.buildrunner/decisions.log`" — this appends only the literal echo string, not the claude -p output.

findings.finding: Phase 2 MCP registration placed in project-scoped `.claude/settings.json` on Muddy only; autopilot dispatches to Otis/Lockwood will not inherit it
findings.claim: A project-scoped settings.json on Muddy is not synced to other nodes, so Claude Code sessions spawned on Otis/Lockwood will not have the repomix MCP server registered and will fail any Repomix MCP tool call.
findings.evidence: Plan states goal: "Any Claude Code session in BuildRunner3 (including autopilot dispatches) can call Repomix MCP tools" — but only creates file at local project path, not at user-level `~/.claude/settings.json` on each node.

findings.finding: Phase 5 `lessons-learned.sh` calls `claude -p` inside a UserPromptSubmit/PostToolUse hook chain, risking recursive hook invocation
findings.claim: Launching `claude -p` from within an autopilot hook fires a new Claude Code session which itself triggers UserPromptSubmit hooks, creating a recursive hook loop or unexpected hook side effects on each lessons capture.
findings.evidence: Plan: "launch `claude -p` with tight 10-line prompt" called from `autopilot-phase-hook.sh`; CLAUDE.md global rules show UserPromptSubmit hook array is active. No guard against re-entrant hook invocation is mentioned anywhere in the plan.

findings.finding: Jimmy `api/memory/note` endpoint existence is asserted by analogy only, not confirmed
findings.claim: Phase 5 will silently fail to write Jimmy memory notes if the endpoint does not exist, with no fallback or detection step described.
findings.evidence: Plan: "Jimmy memory note: `POST $NODE_URL/api/memory/note` (pattern from `/save` skill)" — phrasing 'pattern from' indicates inference, not verification. No Phase 5 deliverable verifies the endpoint before relying on it.

findings.finding: Phase 6 Repomix bundle runs pre-phase on files listed as NEW in the BUILD spec — those files do not yet exist
findings.claim: When `repomix-bundle.sh` parses `**Files:**` entries marked NEW and passes them to `repomix --compress --include`, repomix will fail or silently produce empty output for non-existent paths, producing a broken or empty context bundle.
findings.evidence: Plan: `repomix-bundle.sh` parses `**Files:**` list for the phase and runs `repomix --compress --include <list> --stdout`; every phase lists NEW files (e.g., Phase 1 lists `repomix-cluster-install.sh (NEW)`). No existence check described.

findings.finding: LLMLingua processing Repomix compressed XML output risks corrupting the structured format
findings.claim: LLMLingua is a natural-language token pruner; applying it to Repomix's XML schema (`<file>`, `<path>`, semantic marker tags) will prune structural tokens needed to parse the bundle, potentially producing malformed XML that corrupts the phase context.
findings.evidence: Plan: "Repomix runs FIRST (AST-aware compression), then LLMLingua (optional prose compression) processes the already-bundled output." No mitigation is described for LLMLingua removing XML structural tokens.

findings.finding: `autopilot-dispatch-prefix.sh` line 115/117 and `dispatch-to-node.sh` lines 534-544/598 and `autopilot-phase-hook.sh` lines 18-21 are fragile line-number references to live files
findings.location: Phase 5 and Phase 6 deliverables referencing line numbers in autopilot scripts
findings.claim: Any edit to these actively maintained scripts since the survey invalidates the stated insertion points, causing modifications to land in wrong locations and silently corrupt script logic.
findings.evidence: Plan cites specific line numbers: "autopilot-dispatch-prefix.sh (insertion point: between line 115 EOF and line 117 LLMLingua block)", "dispatch-to-node.sh (posture assembly lines 534-544; post-SSH hook point after line 598)", "autopilot-phase-hook.sh (current early-exit lines 18-21)". No content hash or anchor-string verification step is included.

findings.finding: /tmp/br3-auto-context-tripped circuit breaker state is shared across all concurrent sessions with no session scoping
findings.claim: One session tripping the circuit breaker writes a global file that silences auto-context in all other concurrent Claude sessions for the remainder of their lifetime.
findings.evidence: Plan: "if Jimmy >500ms 3x in a row, skip for remainder of session (state in `/tmp/br3-auto-context-tripped`)" — filename has no session ID or PID component; multiple autopilot dispatches share /tmp on Muddy.

findings.finding: Circuit breaker state file `/tmp/br3-auto-context-tripped` has no TTL and persists across sessions until manually deleted
findings.claim: A transient Jimmy slowdown will permanently disable auto-context in all future sessions until the file is manually removed, with no self-healing mechanism.
findings.evidence: Plan describes creating the file on 3x timeout but no cleanup step: no deletion on session start, no expiry timestamp, no cron cleanup. Plan says "skip for remainder of session" but file persists beyond session boundaries.

findings.finding: Phase 4 references 'six explicit cat calls' in `/cluster-research` Step 1 lines 20-32 without confirming current file content
findings.claim: If the file has been modified since the survey, the cat calls at lines 20-32 may not exist, may be different in number, or may already use a different mechanism, making the replacement step target wrong lines.
findings.evidence: Plan: "replace the six explicit `cat` calls with a single `repomix` call" — no file excerpt is provided in cited excerpts; file content is not verified in Phase 4 deliverables. Survey is a separate artifact not reproduced.

findings.finding: Phase 6 `repomix-bundle.sh` parses `**Files:**` Markdown from BUILD specs using shell scripting with no sanitization — paths passed directly to repomix CLI
findings.claim: A BUILD spec file path containing shell metacharacters (spaces, semicolons, backticks) would result in command injection when passed to `repomix --compress --include <list>`.
findings.evidence: Plan: "parses `**Files:**` list for the phase, runs `repomix --compress --include <list> --stdout`" — no quoting, escaping, or validation of extracted path strings is described anywhere in the deliverables.

findings.finding: Phase 4 repomix glob replacement for specific cat-called files may match unintended files
findings.claim: Using `--include <6-file-glob>` instead of explicit file paths risks including additional files that match the glob, inflating context size rather than reducing it.
findings.evidence: Plan: "replace the six explicit `cat` calls with a single `repomix --compress --include <6-file-glob> --stdout`" — six specific files are unlikely to share a clean unique glob pattern; the plan provides no glob validation step.

findings.finding: `command -v repomix` fallback detection in dispatched remote skills is unreliable
findings.claim: Skills execute in the Claude Code session's shell environment, which may not inherit the same PATH as the node's login shell where repomix was installed; `command -v repomix` may return empty even when repomix is installed, silently falling back when it shouldn't or vice versa.
findings.evidence: Plan: "fallback to per-file Read when `repomix` binary missing (detect via `command -v repomix`)" — npm global installs on macOS go to `/usr/local/bin` or nvm paths; Claude Code sessions may run in a restricted PATH.

findings.finding: Phase 1 Windows Below SSH + PowerShell npm install branch unspecified
findings.claim: SSH to a Windows node requires OpenSSH server to be pre-configured, and `npm install -g repomix` in PowerShell behaves differently regarding PATH updates; no setup prerequisite check or PowerShell-specific install command is described, risking silent failure on Below.
findings.evidence: Plan: "per-node SSH loop (Windows Below via PowerShell branch)" — this is the only reference to the Windows branch; no PowerShell command, no OpenSSH prerequisite, no PATH reload step mentioned.

findings.finding: Phase 3 A1 flipping `BR3_AUTO_CONTEXT=on` in `~/.bashrc`/`~/.zshrc` affects all projects on the machine, not just BR3
findings.claim: Modifying global shell init files to enable a BR3-specific behavior flag contradicts the stated scope of 'changes apply across every BR3 project' by also affecting non-BR3 projects if their hooks check the same variable.
findings.evidence: Plan: "Flip `BR3_AUTO_CONTEXT=on` in `~/.bashrc` / `~/.zshrc`" — global shell env sets this for every shell session regardless of project context.

findings.finding: Phase 3 success criterion '100 consecutive prompts show 0 blocks / no latency regression >500ms median' has no defined measurement methodology
findings.claim: The criterion is stated as a verification gate but no tool, script, or data source for measuring 'consecutive prompts' and 'median latency' is described, making it unverifiable.
findings.evidence: Phase 3: "100 consecutive prompts in real session show 0 blocks / no latency regression >500ms median" — no logging, timing instrumentation, or data capture mechanism is included in Phase 3 deliverables.

findings.finding: Phase 6 success criterion 'file-reads drop by ≥30%' has no automated measurement mechanism
findings.claim: Counting mid-session file-reads from a transcript requires manual transcript inspection; no script or tooling is described to automate this measurement.
findings.evidence: Phase 6: "measured phase mid-session file-reads drop by ≥30% on at least 1 phase; no regression in phase success" — no transcript parsing tool or metric-capture step in deliverables.

findings.finding: Phase 7 token count comparison has no automated measurement tool specified
findings.claim: The verification report comparing token/latency vs baseline cannot be produced without a tool to count tokens in phase prompts, which is not described in any deliverable.
findings.evidence: Phase 7: "Write verification report comparing token/latency vs a baseline dry-run with all flags off" — no tokenizer or Claude API token-count call is included in Phase 7 deliverables.

findings.finding: Phase 2 MCP registration uses unpinned `npx -y repomix --mcp` with no version constraint
findings.claim: An unpinned npx invocation fetches whatever repomix version is current at session start, allowing a silent breaking change in the MCP server interface to affect all sessions without notice.
findings.evidence: Plan: "`mcpServers.repomix = { command: "npx", args: ["-y", "repomix", "--mcp"] }`" — no `@<version>` pin on the package name.

findings.finding: Phase 3 probe cost budget $0.30 has no enforcement mechanism
findings.claim: The $0.30 cost ceiling is listed as a constraint but no abort-on-budget-exceeded logic is described, so probe overruns are possible.
findings.evidence: Phase 3: "LLM judge (Sonnet 4.6, effort medium, ≤$0.30 total)" — no token budget check, API cost tracking, or early-exit condition in the probe script deliverable.

findings.finding: Phase 1 execution trigger (manual vs automated) is ambiguous
findings.claim: Deliverables list both 'Write repomix-cluster-install.sh' and 'Execute install on all 7 nodes' as separate checklist items without specifying whether execution is triggered by running the script or requires manual SSH per node.
findings.evidence: Phase 1: "[ ] Write `repomix-cluster-install.sh`" then "[ ] Execute install on all 7 nodes" — the gap between writing and executing is unaddressed; if the script is the trigger, why is execution a separate deliverable?

findings.finding: Phase 7 partial failure leaves decisions.log entries and Jimmy memory notes uncleaned
findings.claim: If Phase 7 fails mid-run, the scratch BUILD spec cleanup step won't execute, and decisions.log and Jimmy memory will contain spurious smoke-test entries with no defined remediation.
findings.evidence: Phase 7: "[ ] Clean up scratch BUILD spec after verification" — this is listed last; no rollback step covers partial Phase 7 execution. Rollback section says only 'Individual flags unset'.

findings.finding: Phase 4 model pin enforcement is described as 'enforced in edits' with no concrete mechanism
findings.claim: Saying the model pin is 'enforced in edits' without specifying that the frontmatter `model: claude-sonnet-4-6` line must not be touched leaves the pin at risk of accidental removal during the edit.
findings.evidence: Phase 4: "Verify model pin stays `claude-sonnet-4-6` on both skills (enforced in edits)" — no diff guard, no grep check, no test assertion on the pin line is specified.

findings.finding: Jimmy `api/memory/note` endpoint has no authentication mentioned for POST calls
findings.claim: Any process on the internal network can write arbitrary memory notes to Jimmy without authentication, which is a low-severity concern on a trusted LAN but represents an unacknowledged design choice.
findings.evidence: Plan: "POST $NODE_URL/api/memory/note" — no auth header, token, or credential is mentioned. Jimmy API port 8100 is documented as open to the cluster.

+## Architecture principle — Claude Code owns ALL reasoning
+
+**Added 2026-04-22 (architecture reconciliation). Binding on every phase below.**
+
+After auditing `~/Projects/Synapse`, it's clear Synapse already contains the framework engine (PAS, PASTOR, StoryBrand, FAB, AIDA modules; Cialdini per-page lever assignment; Stadium Pitch Smoking Gun; 90/10 education-pitch gate; customer-as-hero gate; VoC verbatim check; persona extraction; driver extraction; vocabulary extraction; insight persistence; competitor analysis). Website-Builder MUST NOT reimplement any of this.
+
+**Division of labor (locked):**
+
+| Layer                   | Role                                             | Does                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Does NOT                                                                                      |
+| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
+| **Synapse**             | Research warehouse + framework canonical library | VoC corpus, insight inventory, personas (4-axis drivers), competitor intel, brand voice (Aaker), framework definitions, research sources, strategic brief                                                                                                                                                       | Compose final website copy (Synapse's `generate-website-copy` is NOT called in this pipeline) |
+| **Claude Code skills**  | Reasoning / copywriter / framework applier       | Schwartz routing, MECLABS scoring, Stadium Pitch composition, Copyhackers 10X modality mapping, LIFT, 4 Forces, Wynter 4-layer, GEO entity architecture, framework selection per page, all headline/body/CTA writing, before/after scoring, change log (Appendix B), unused-intelligence inventory (Appendix A) | Mine VoC, extract personas, scrape competitors, rewrite Synapse logic                         |
+| **Website-Builder app** | UI + orchestration + mechanical proxies          | UI screens, stage transitions, queue dispatch, human review/edit, data proxies to Synapse, writes to disk/DB                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Anything that needs judgment; any LLM call from an edge function                              |
+
+**Edge-function rule (hard gate — the 5 allowed jobs):** Website-Builder edge functions do only:
+
+1. **Auth + RLS gates** — validate the caller, set service role context
+2. **DB CRUD** — insert / update / read Supabase rows
+3. **External proxies with secrets** — call Synapse (or other vendor) with the service key, retry, map errors
+4. **Deterministic transforms** — zod validate, rule-based field mapping, UUID generation
+5. **Queue dispatch** — insert into `cli_queue`, return `{ command_id }`
+
+No edge function may call an LLM. Any edge function that currently does so MUST be converted to a CLI skill (see Phase 6). Exception: embedding-generation endpoints used for semantic search (deterministic model call, no composition).
+
+**Reasoning-creep anti-patterns (watch-outs):** These smell small but ARE reasoning — call them out if they sneak in:
+
+- **Classification calls** ("is this industry SaaS or services?") — CLI skill, or extract as deterministic rules
+- **Structured extraction from free text** via LLM — CLI skill (zod mapping is mechanical and OK)
+- **Summarization before storage** — CLI skill
+- **Model pinning** (`MODEL = 'claude-...'` inside an edge fn) — always reasoning, always migrate
+
+**Embedding generation** for semantic search is the single whitelisted exception because the call is deterministic (vector-in, vector-out), bounded, and no composition happens.
+
+**Hand-authored target:** `.buildrunner/content/post_synapse_content.md` — the operator-authored reference document demonstrating the output shape Claude Code must produce. Every framework it applies (Schwartz, MECLABS, Chet Holmes Stadium Pitch + Buying Criteria Reset + Buyer's Pyramid, Wynter 4-layer, Copyhackers 10X modalities, LIFT, 4 Forces of Progress, PAS/BAB/AIDA/FAB/StoryBrand/PASTOR selection, GEO entity architecture, risk reversal, Grunt Test, readability targeting, Appendix A/B change logging) is in scope for `/enrich-content`.
+
+**Two pipeline paths, one reasoning engine:**
+
+- **Full path** (workfloDock or quick form intake): triggers full Synapse pipeline → sync-synapse-bundle proxies all 8 raw-data endpoints → `/enrich-content` composes `post-synapse.json` → `/website-build` renders.
+- **Wizard-lite path** (redesign wizard or first-design wizard): triggers `intake-strategic-spine-lite` (Synapse-side, thinner brief) → same 8 proxies, thinner payloads → same `/enrich-content` skill, narrower ammunition → `/website-build` renders.  ## Authoring principle — reuse first
 
 Every phase lists **Reuse**, **Modify**, **Extract**, **New**, and **Disposition** subsections. A file is only `New` when no existing implementation or analog exists in the repo. `Extract` means moving code out of an existing large file into a dedicated module (with deletion of the original block). `Disposition` enumerates legacy files/columns/migrations that die or are renamed as a direct consequence of the phase.

+import type { ResearchKindEntry } from '../../hooks/useDesignProfile';
+import { ResearchPanelShell } from './ResearchPanelShell';
+
+export interface BrandDNAPayload {
+  headline_tone?: string;
+  density_score?: number;
+  heading_structure?: string;
+  fonts?: string[];
+  palette?: Array<{ hex: string; role?: string }>;
+  voice?: string;
+  sourceUrl?: string;
+  summary?: string;
+}
+
+export interface BrandDNAPanelProps {
+  entry: ResearchKindEntry | undefined;
+  onRerun: () => void;
+  rerunDisabled?: boolean;
+}
+
+function readPayload(entry: ResearchKindEntry | undefined): BrandDNAPayload | null {
+  if (!entry?.payload || typeof entry.payload !== 'object') return null;
+  return entry.payload as BrandDNAPayload;
+}
+
+export function BrandDNAPanel({ entry, onRerun, rerunDisabled }: BrandDNAPanelProps) {
+  const payload = readPayload(entry);
+
+  return (
+    <ResearchPanelShell
+      title="Brand DNA"
+      description="Tone, heading structure, and visual density extracted from the brand's current surface."
+      entry={entry}
+      onRerun={onRerun}
+      rerunDisabled={rerunDisabled}
+    >
+      {payload?.summary ? (
+        <p className="text-sm leading-6" style={{ color: 'var(--text-secondary)' }}>
+          {payload.summary}
+        </p>
+      ) : null}
+
+      <div className="grid gap-3 md:grid-cols-3">
+        {payload?.headline_tone ? (
+          <MetaCell label="Headline tone" value={payload.headline_tone} />
+        ) : null}
+        {typeof payload?.density_score === 'number' ? (
+          <MetaCell label="Density score" value={`${payload.density_score.toFixed(2)} / 1.0`} />
+        ) : null}
+        {payload?.heading_structure ? (
+          <MetaCell label="Heading structure" value={payload.heading_structure} />
+        ) : null}
+      </div>
+
+      {payload?.palette && payload.palette.length > 0 ? (
+        <div className="space-y-2">
+          <div
+            className="text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.16em]"
+            style={{ color: 'var(--text-muted)' }}
+          >
+            Palette
+          </div>
+          <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
+            {payload.palette.map((color) => (
+              <div
+                key={`${color.hex}-${color.role ?? 'color'}`}
+                className="flex items-center gap-2 rounded-lg border px-2 py-1 text-xs"
+                style={{ borderColor: 'var(--border)', color: 'var(--text-secondary)' }}
+              >
+                <span
+                  className="h-4 w-4 rounded border"
+                  style={{ backgroundColor: color.hex, borderColor: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.12)' }}
+                />
+                {color.hex}
+                {color.role ? ` · ${color.role}` : ''}
+              </div>
+            ))}
+          </div>
+        </div>
+      ) : null}
+
+      {payload?.fonts && payload.fonts.length > 0 ? (
+        <div>
+          <div
+            className="text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.16em]"
+            style={{ color: 'var(--text-muted)' }}
+          >
+            Fonts
+          </div>
+          <div className="mt-1 text-sm" style={{ color: 'var(--text-primary)' }}>
+            {payload.fonts.join(', ')}
+          </div>
+        </div>
+      ) : null}
+
+      {payload?.voice ? (
+        <div>
+          <div
+            className="text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.16em]"
+            style={{ color: 'var(--text-muted)' }}
+          >
+            Voice
+          </div>
+          <p className="mt-1 text-sm leading-6" style={{ color: 'var(--text-secondary)' }}>
+            {payload.voice}
+          </p>
+        </div>
+      ) : null}
+
+      {payload?.sourceUrl ? (
+        <div className="text-xs" style={{ color: 'var(--text-muted)' }}>
+          Source:{' '}
+          <a
+            href={payload.sourceUrl}
+            target="_blank"
+            rel="noreferrer"
+            className="underline"
+            style={{ color: 'var(--accent)' }}
+          >
+            {payload.sourceUrl}
+          </a>
+        </div>
+      ) : null}
+
+      {!payload ? (
+        <p className="text-sm" style={{ color: 'var(--text-muted)' }}>
+          No brand DNA extracted yet.
+        </p>
+      ) : null}
+    </ResearchPanelShell>
+  );
+}
+
+function MetaCell({ label, value }: { label: string; value: string }) {
+  return (
+    <div className="rounded-xl border p-3" style={{ borderColor: 'var(--border)' }}>
+      <div className="text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.16em]" style={{ color: 'var(--text-muted)' }}>
+        {label}
+      </div>
+      <div className="mt-1 text-sm" style={{ color: 'var(--text-primary)' }}>
+        {value}
+      </div>
+    </div>
+  );
+}

diff --git a/src/components/research/DifferentiationPanel.tsx b/src/components/research/DifferentiationPanel.tsx
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..677c1c0
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/components/research/DifferentiationPanel.tsx
@@ -0,0 +1,185 @@
+import { useMemo } from 'react';
+import type { ResearchKindEntry } from '../../hooks/useDesignProfile';
+import type { CompetitorVisualProfile } from '../../types/design-reasoning';
+import { ResearchPanelShell } from './ResearchPanelShell';
+import type { BrandDNAPayload } from './BrandDNAPanel';
+import type { ConventionSummaryPayload } from './ConventionSummary';
+import type { CompetitorGridPayload } from './CompetitorGrid';
+
+export interface DifferentiationPanelProps {
+  /** Virtual entry (always 'ready' once synthesized) — status surfaces here. */
+  entry: ResearchKindEntry | undefined;
+  conventionsEntry: ResearchKindEntry | undefined;
+  competitorsEntry: ResearchKindEntry | undefined;
+  brandDnaEntry: ResearchKindEntry | undefined;
+  fallbackCompetitorProfiles?: CompetitorVisualProfile[];
+  onRerun: () => void;
+  rerunDisabled?: boolean;
+}
+
+function readConventions(entry: ResearchKindEntry | undefined): ConventionSummaryPayload | null {
+  if (!entry?.payload || typeof entry.payload !== 'object') return null;
+  return entry.payload as ConventionSummaryPayload;
+}
+
+function readCompetitors(entry: ResearchKindEntry | undefined): CompetitorGridPayload | null {
+  if (!entry?.payload || typeof entry.payload !== 'object') return null;
+  return entry.payload as CompetitorGridPayload;
+}
+
+function readBrandDna(entry: ResearchKindEntry | undefined): BrandDNAPayload | null {
+  if (!entry?.payload || typeof entry.payload !== 'object') return null;
+  return entry.payload as BrandDNAPayload;
+}
+
+interface Synthesis {
+  positioningGaps: string[];
+  whitespaceOpportunities: string[];
+  categoryNorms: string[];
+}
+
+/**
+ * Synthesize a differentiation view from the other three kinds. Runs client-side
+ * so the panel becomes meaningful the moment its dependencies land — no extra
+ * edge-fn round trip.
+ */
+function synthesize(
+  conventions: ConventionSummaryPayload | null,
+  competitorsPayload: CompetitorGridPayload | null,
+  fallbackCompetitors: CompetitorVisualProfile[],
+  brandDna: BrandDNAPayload | null
+): Synthesis {
+  const profiles =
+    competitorsPayload?.profiles && competitorsPayload.profiles.length > 0
+      ? competitorsPayload.profiles
+      : fallbackCompetitors;
+
+  const categoryNorms = conventions?.conventions?.map((convention) => convention.label) ?? [];
+
+  const whitespaceSet = new Set<string>();
+  for (const profile of profiles) {
+    for (const opportunity of profile.differentiationOpportunities ?? []) {
+      if (opportunity.trim()) whitespaceSet.add(opportunity.trim());
+    }
+  }
+
+  const positioningGaps: string[] = [];
+  if (brandDna?.headline_tone && categoryNorms.length > 0) {
+    positioningGaps.push(
+      `Brand tone "${brandDna.headline_tone}" vs category norms: ${categoryNorms.slice(0, 3).join(', ')}.`
+    );
+  }
+  if (profiles.length > 0) {
+    const heroes = new Set(profiles.map((p) => p.heroType).filter(Boolean));
+    if (heroes.size <= 2) {
+      positioningGaps.push(
+        `Hero conformity: ${[...heroes].join(', ')} — visual split here is possible.`
+      );
+    }
+    const ctas = new Set(profiles.map((p) => p.ctaStyle).filter(Boolean));
+    if (ctas.size <= 2) {
+      positioningGaps.push(
+        `CTA conformity: ${[...ctas].join(', ')} — operator can break with a contrarian CTA.`
+      );
+    }
+  }
+
+  return {
+    positioningGaps,
+    whitespaceOpportunities: [...whitespaceSet].slice(0, 8),
+    categoryNorms: categoryNorms.slice(0, 8),
+  };
+}
+
+export function DifferentiationPanel({
+  entry,
+  conventionsEntry,
+  competitorsEntry,
+  brandDnaEntry,
+  fallbackCompetitorProfiles,
+  onRerun,
+  rerunDisabled,
+}: DifferentiationPanelProps) {
+  const conventions = readConventions(conventionsEntry);
+  const competitors = readCompetitors(competitorsEntry);
+  const brandDna = readBrandDna(brandDnaEntry);
+
+  const synthesis = useMemo(
+    () => synthesize(conventions, competitors, fallbackCompetitorProfiles ?? [], brandDna),
+    [conventions, competitors, fallbackCompetitorProfiles, brandDna]
+  );
+
+  const hasAnything =
+    synthesis.positioningGaps.length > 0 ||
+    synthesis.whitespaceOpportunities.length > 0 ||
+    synthesis.categoryNorms.length > 0;
+
+  return (
+    <ResearchPanelShell
+      title="Differentiation opportunities"
+      description="Synthesized from conventions, competitors, and brand DNA — no extra dispatch."
+      entry={entry}
+      onRerun={onRerun}
+      rerunDisabled={rerunDisabled}
+    >
+      {!hasAnything ? (
+        <p className="text-sm" style={{ color: 'var(--text-muted)' }}>
+          Waiting on conventions and competitors — this view fills in automatically.
+        </p>
+      ) : null}
+
+      {synthesis.positioningGaps.length > 0 ? (
+        <Section title="Positioning gaps">
+          <ul className="space-y-1 text-sm" style={{ color: 'var(--text-secondary)' }}>
+            {synthesis.positioningGaps.map((gap, index) => (
+              <li key={`gap-${index}`}>• {gap}</li>
+            ))}
+          </ul>
+        </Section>
+      ) : null}
+
+      {synthesis.whitespaceOpportunities.length > 0 ? (
+        <Section title="Whitespace opportunities">
+          <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
+            {synthesis.whitespaceOpportunities.map((opportunity, index) => (
+              <span
+                key={`ws-${index}`}
+                className="rounded-full border px-3 py-1 text-xs"
+                style={{ borderColor: 'var(--border)', color: 'var(--text-secondary)' }}
+              >
+                {opportunity}
+              </span>
+            ))}
+          </div>
+        </Section>
+      ) : null}
+
+      {synthesis.categoryNorms.length > 0 ? (
+        <Section title="Category norms to react to">
+          <div className="flex flex-wrap gap-2">
+            {synthesis.categoryNorms.map((norm, index) => (
+              <span
+                key={`norm-${index}`}
+                className="rounded-full border px-3 py-1 text-xs"
+                style={{ borderColor: 'var(--border)', color: 'var(--text-muted)' }}
+              >
+                {norm}
+              </span>
+            ))}
+          </div>
+        </Section>
+      ) : null}
+    </ResearchPanelShell>
+  );
+}
+
+function Section({ title, children }: { title: string; children: React.ReactNode }) {
+  return (
+    <div className="space-y-2">
+      <div className="text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.16em]" style={{ color: 'var(--text-muted)' }}>
+        {title}
+      </div>
+      {children}
+    </div>
+  );
+}

+import type { ReactNode } from 'react';
+import { AlertTriangle, Loader2, RotateCcw } from 'lucide-react';
+import { Badge } from '../ui/badge';
+import { Button } from '../ui/button';
+import { Card, CardContent, CardDescription, CardHeader, CardTitle } from '../ui/card';
+import type { ResearchKindEntry, ResearchKindStatus } from '../../hooks/useDesignProfile';
+
+export interface ResearchPanelShellProps {
+  title: string;
+  description: string;
+  entry: ResearchKindEntry | undefined;
+  onRerun: () => void;
+  rerunDisabled?: boolean;
+  children: ReactNode;
+}
+
+const STATUS_TONE: Record<ResearchKindStatus, 'info' | 'success' | 'warning'> = {
+  pending: 'info',
+  running: 'info',
+  ready: 'success',
+  failed: 'warning',
+};
+
+function statusLabel(status: ResearchKindStatus | undefined): string {
+  if (!status) return 'Not dispatched';
+  if (status === 'running') return 'Running…';
+  if (status === 'ready') return 'Ready';
+  if (status === 'failed') return 'Failed';
+  return 'Pending';
+}
+
+export function ResearchPanelShell({
+  title,
+  description,
+  entry,
+  onRerun,
+  rerunDisabled,
+  children,
+}: ResearchPanelShellProps) {
+  const status = entry?.status;
+  const tone = status ? STATUS_TONE[status] : 'info';
+  const isLoading = status === 'pending' || status === 'running' || !status;
+
+  return (
+    <Card style={{ backgroundColor: 'var(--surface-2)', borderColor: 'var(--border)' }}>
+      <CardHeader>
+        <div className="flex flex-wrap items-start justify-between gap-3">
+          <div>
+            <CardTitle style={{ color: 'var(--text-primary)' }}>{title}</CardTitle>
+            <CardDescription style={{ color: 'var(--text-muted)' }}>{description}</CardDescription>
+          </div>
+          <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
+            <Badge variant={tone}>{statusLabel(status)}</Badge>
+            <Button
+              type="button"
+              variant="outline"
+              size="sm"
+              onClick={onRerun}
+              disabled={rerunDisabled}
+            >
+              {status === 'running' ? (
+                <Loader2 size={14} className="animate-spin" />
+              ) : (
+                <RotateCcw size={14} />
+              )}
+              Rerun
+            </Button>
+          </div>
+        </div>
+      </CardHeader>
+      <CardContent className="space-y-4 pt-2">
+        {entry?.error ? (
+          <div
+            className="flex items-start gap-2 rounded-xl border border-amber-500/30 bg-amber-500/10 px-4 py-3 text-sm text-amber-200"
+            role="alert"
+          >
+            <AlertTriangle size={14} className="mt-0.5" />
+            <span>{entry.error}</span>
+          </div>
+        ) : null}
+        {isLoading ? <ResearchPanelSkeleton /> : children}
+      </CardContent>
+    </Card>
+  );
+}
+
+export function ResearchPanelSkeleton() {
+  return (
+    <div className="space-y-3" aria-hidden>
+      <div className="h-4 w-3/4 animate-pulse rounded bg-white/5" />
+      <div className="h-4 w-1/2 animate-pulse rounded bg-white/5" />
+      <div className="h-24 w-full animate-pulse rounded bg-white/5" />
+    </div>
+  );
+}

+ * realtime updates on `design_research`, and exposes a `rerun(kind)` helper.
+ *
+ * Race gates (R-3 in BUILD spec):
+ *   - AbortController on unmount — short-circuits mid-flight ensureRequiredResearch loops.
+ *   - removeChannel on unmount — no leaked realtime subscription.
+ *   - edge fns dedupe via `research_kinds[kind].status === 'running' | 'ready'`.
+ *
+ * The hook does NOT read from the design_research row directly — it trusts
+ * useDesignProfile to surface `researchKinds`. It takes the current snapshot as
+ * input so dispatch decisions are driven by the same projection the UI sees.
+ */

+export interface UseResearchKickoffOptions {
+  projectId: string | null | undefined;
+  requiredKinds: EnsureResearchKind[];
+  researchKinds: Record<string, ResearchKindEntry>;
+  dispatchOptions?: Omit<EnsureResearchOptions, 'signal' | 'force'>;
+  /** Disable auto-dispatch on mount (useful for tests or manual-only flows). */
+  autoDispatch?: boolean;
+}
+
+export type KickoffPhase = 'idle' | 'dispatching' | 'subscribed' | 'error';
+
+export interface UseResearchKickoffResult {
+  phase: KickoffPhase;
+  lastResult: EnsureRequiredResearchResult | null;
+  error: string | null;
+  rerun: (kind: EnsureResearchKind) => Promise<void>;
+}
+
+export function useResearchKickoff(options: UseResearchKickoffOptions): UseResearchKickoffResult {
+  const { projectId, requiredKinds, researchKinds, dispatchOptions, autoDispatch = true } = options;
+
+  const [phase, setPhase] = useState<KickoffPhase>('idle');
+  const [lastResult, setLastResult] = useState<EnsureRequiredResearchResult | null>(null);
+  const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
+
+  // Latest snapshot of researchKinds — read by rerun without re-binding.
+  const researchKindsRef = useRef(researchKinds);
+  researchKindsRef.current = researchKinds;
+
+  const dispatchOptionsRef = useRef(dispatchOptions);
+  dispatchOptionsRef.current = dispatchOptions;
+
+  // Single AbortController per mount; cancelled in cleanup.
+  const abortRef = useRef<AbortController | null>(null);
+
+  const rerun = useCallback(
+    async (kind: EnsureResearchKind) => {
+      if (!projectId) return;
+      const signal = abortRef.current?.signal;
+      if (signal?.aborted) return;
+
+      setPhase('dispatching');
+      setError(null);
+      try {
+        const result = await ensureRequiredResearch(projectId, [kind], researchKindsRef.current, {
+          ...dispatchOptionsRef.current,
+          force: true,
+          signal,
+        });
+        if (signal?.aborted) return;
+        setLastResult(result);
+        setPhase(result.failed.length > 0 ? 'error' : 'subscribed');
+        if (result.failed.length > 0) {
+          setError(result.failed.map((entry) => `${entry.kind}: ${entry.error}`).join('; '));
+        }
+      } catch (err) {
+        if (signal?.aborted) return;
+        setPhase('error');
+        setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err));
+      }
+    },
+    [projectId]
+  );
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    if (!projectId) {
+      setPhase('idle');
+      return;
+    }
+
+    const controller = new AbortController();
+    abortRef.current = controller;
+    const { signal } = controller;
+
+    let channel: RealtimeChannel | null = null;
+
+    const subscribe = () => {
+      if (signal.aborted) return;
+      channel = supabase
+        .channel(`research_kickoff:${projectId}`)
+        .on(
+          'postgres_changes',
+          {
+            event: 'UPDATE',
+            schema: 'public',
+            table: 'design_research',
+            filter: `project_id=eq.${projectId}`,
+          },
+          () => {
+            if (signal.aborted) return;
+            // No local state read — useDesignProfile consumers get the updated
+            // `researchKinds` via their own subscription. This channel exists
+            // to keep the kickoff hook's lifecycle observable and testable.
+          }
+        )
+        .subscribe((status) => {
+          if (signal.aborted) return;
+          if (status === 'SUBSCRIBED') setPhase('subscribed');
+        });
+    };
+
+    const dispatch = async () => {
+      if (!autoDispatch) {
+        subscribe();
+        return;
+      }
+
+      setPhase('dispatching');
+      setError(null);
+      try {
+        const result = await ensureRequiredResearch(
+          projectId,
+          requiredKinds,
+          researchKindsRef.current,
+          { ...dispatchOptionsRef.current, signal }
+        );
+        if (signal.aborted) return;
+        setLastResult(result);
+        if (result.failed.length > 0) {
+          setPhase('error');
+          setError(result.failed.map((entry) => `${entry.kind}: ${entry.error}`).join('; '));
+        }
+        subscribe();
+      } catch (err) {
+        if (signal.aborted) return;
+        setPhase('error');
+        setError(err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err));
+      }
+    };
+
+    void dispatch();
+
+    return () => {
+      controller.abort();
+      abortRef.current = null;
+      if (channel) {
+        void supabase.removeChannel(channel);
+        channel = null;
+      }
+    };
+    // requiredKinds identity changing is rare — consumers should pass a stable
+    // reference. We intentionally don't re-dispatch on every render; the
+    // effect only re-runs when projectId or autoDispatch flip.
+    // eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps
+  }, [projectId, autoDispatch]);
+
+  return { phase, lastResult, error, rerun };
+}

+// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// Phase 4 — Research kind dispatch + idempotent ensureRequiredResearch.
+//
+// Each research kind maps to a thin edge function that inserts a row into
+// `cli_queue`. The CLI skill writes back to `design_research.research_kinds`
+// using the shape { status, payload, error, updated_at }, keyed by kind.

+// Kinds required by the Research stage:
+//   'conventions'     — industry-convention summary
+//   'brand-dna'       — populated by extract-brand-dna (redesign path only)
+//   'proof-points'    — ranked proof evidence
+//   'competitors'     — legacy competitor research
+//   'differentiation' — derived from conventions + competitors + brand-dna (client-side for now)
+// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+export type EnsureResearchKind = ResearchKind;
+
+export interface EnsureResearchOptions {
+  /** Force re-dispatch even if kind status is 'running' or 'ready'. Default false. */
+  force?: boolean;
+  projectSlug?: string;
+  ownerId?: string;
+  designResearchId?: string;
+  /** Only used by 'brand-dna' kind — URL to extract from. Required for that kind. */
+  sourceUrl?: string;
+  /** Only used by 'competitors' kind — passthrough override URL. */
+  competitorUrl?: string;
+  competitorIndex?: number;
+  /** AbortSignal to short-circuit in-flight dispatches. */
+  signal?: AbortSignal;
+}
+
+interface SupabaseInvokeResponse {
+  status?: 'queued' | 'skipped' | string;
+  command_id?: string;
+  reason?: string;
+  error?: string;
+}
+
+function edgeFnNameForKind(kind: EnsureResearchKind): string | null {
+  switch (kind) {
+    case 'conventions':
+      return 'research-conventions';
+    case 'brand-dna':
+      return 'extract-brand-dna';
+    case 'proof-points':
+      return 'research-proof-points';
+    case 'competitors':
+      return 'research-competitors';
+    case 'differentiation':
+      // Derived client-side from other kinds; no edge fn dispatch.
+      return null;
+  }
+}
+
+async function dispatchResearchKind(
+  kind: EnsureResearchKind,
+  projectId: string,
+  options: EnsureResearchOptions
+): Promise<SupabaseInvokeResponse> {
+  if (options.signal?.aborted) {
+    throw new Error('Research dispatch aborted');
+  }
+
+  const fnName = edgeFnNameForKind(kind);
+  if (!fnName) {
+    return { status: 'skipped', reason: 'kind is derived client-side' };
+  }
+
+  const body: Record<string, unknown> = {
+    project_id: projectId,
+    project_slug: options.projectSlug,
+    owner_id: options.ownerId,
+    design_research_id: options.designResearchId,
+    force: options.force ?? false,
+  };
+
+  if (kind === 'brand-dna') {
+    if (!options.sourceUrl) {
+      throw new Error("ensureRequiredResearch: 'brand-dna' requires sourceUrl");
+    }
+    body.url = options.sourceUrl;
+  }
+
+  if (kind === 'competitors') {
+    body.competitor_url = options.competitorUrl ?? null;
+    body.competitor_index = options.competitorIndex ?? null;
+  }
+
+  const { data, error } = await supabase.functions.invoke(fnName, { body });
+  if (error) {
+    throw new Error(`${fnName} invoke failed: ${error.message}`);
+  }
+  return (data ?? {}) as SupabaseInvokeResponse;
+}
+
+export interface EnsureRequiredResearchResult {
+  dispatched: EnsureResearchKind[];
+  skipped: EnsureResearchKind[];
+  failed: Array<{ kind: EnsureResearchKind; error: string }>;
+}
+
+/**
+ * Idempotent dispatch: for each kind, if its current `research_kinds[kind].status`
+ * is 'ready' or 'running' (and `force` is false), skip. Otherwise invoke the
+ * corresponding edge function. Edge functions apply their own idempotency gates
+ * against `cli_queue`, so a double-dispatch in flight is still safe.
+ */
+export async function ensureRequiredResearch(
+  projectId: string,
+  kinds: EnsureResearchKind[],
+  currentStatuses: Record<string, ResearchKindEntry | undefined>,
+  options: EnsureResearchOptions = {}
+): Promise<EnsureRequiredResearchResult> {
+  const dispatched: EnsureResearchKind[] = [];
+  const skipped: EnsureResearchKind[] = [];
+  const failed: Array<{ kind: EnsureResearchKind; error: string }> = [];
+
+  for (const kind of kinds) {
+    if (options.signal?.aborted) break;
+
+    const entry = currentStatuses[kind];
+    const status = entry?.status;
+    if (!options.force && (status === 'ready' || status === 'running')) {
+      skipped.push(kind);
+      continue;
+    }
+
+    const fnName = edgeFnNameForKind(kind);
+    if (!fnName) {
+      skipped.push(kind);
+      continue;
+    }
+
+    try {
+      const response = await dispatchResearchKind(kind, projectId, options);
+      if (response.status === 'skipped') {
+        skipped.push(kind);
+      } else {
+        dispatched.push(kind);
+      }
+    } catch (error) {
+      failed.push({
+        kind,
+        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
+      });
+    }
+  }
+
+  return { dispatched, skipped, failed };
+}

diff --git a/supabase/functions/_shared/researchKinds.ts b/supabase/functions/_shared/researchKinds.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..89f698a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/supabase/functions/_shared/researchKinds.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+/**
+ * Shared helpers for the Phase 4 per-kind research dispatcher pattern.
+ *
+ * Three edge fns (research-conventions, research-competitors,
+ * research-proof-points) all:
+ *   1. Guard against double-dispatch via `design_research.research_kinds.<kind>.status`
+ *   2. Flip the kind to 'running' in the same JSONB column
+ *   3. Enqueue a matching CLI command
+ *
+ * Keeping this logic in one place means all three fns behave identically and
+ * migrations to a different persistence model only touch this file.
+ */
+
+// deno-lint-ignore-file no-explicit-any
+import { createClient, type SupabaseClient } from 'https://esm.sh/@supabase/supabase-js@2';
+
+export type ResearchKindStatus = 'pending' | 'running' | 'ready' | 'failed';
+
+export interface ResearchKindEntry {
+  status: ResearchKindStatus;
+  payload: unknown;
+  error: string | null;
+  updated_at: string;
+}
+
+export type ResearchKindsMap = Record<string, ResearchKindEntry>;
+
+export function getServiceClient(): SupabaseClient {
+  const supabaseUrl = Deno.env.get('SUPABASE_URL');
+  const supabaseServiceKey = Deno.env.get('SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY');
+  if (!supabaseUrl || !supabaseServiceKey) {
+    throw new Error('SUPABASE_URL + SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY must be configured');
+  }
+  return createClient(supabaseUrl, supabaseServiceKey);
+}
+
+export async function readResearchKinds(
+  supabase: SupabaseClient,
+  projectId: string
+): Promise<ResearchKindsMap> {
+  const { data } = await supabase
+    .from('design_research')
+    .select('research_kinds')
+    .eq('project_id', projectId)
+    .maybeSingle();
+  return (data?.research_kinds as ResearchKindsMap | null) ?? {};
+}
+
+export async function markKindRunning(
+  supabase: SupabaseClient,
+  projectId: string,
+  kind: string
+): Promise<void> {
+  const kinds = await readResearchKinds(supabase, projectId);
+  const next: ResearchKindsMap = {
+    ...kinds,
+    [kind]: {
+      status: 'running',
+      payload: null,
+      error: null,
+      updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
+    },
+  };
+  await supabase
+    .from('design_research')
+    .update({ research_kinds: next })
+    .eq('project_id', projectId);
+}
+
+export async function currentKindStatus(
+  supabase: SupabaseClient,
+  projectId: string,
+  kind: string
+): Promise<ResearchKindStatus | null> {
+  const kinds = await readResearchKinds(supabase, projectId);
+  return kinds[kind]?.status ?? null;
+}

+/**
+ * research-competitors — Thin edge function for the Phase 4 research stage.
+ *
+ * Queues a /research-competitors CLI command that expands the competitor
+ * profile set. The CLI skill uses server-side HTML fetch (no headless)
+ * to extract title/meta/h1/CTA + inline-style accent colors, then merges
+ * those into the existing competitor_profiles set.
+ *
+ * Idempotency: skips re-enqueue when research_kinds.competitors.status is
+ * 'running' or 'ready' unless `force` is true.
+ */
+
+import { serve } from 'https://deno.land/std@0.168.0/http/server.ts';
+import { withDevLogs, devLog } from '../_shared/devLog.ts';
+import { currentKindStatus, getServiceClient, markKindRunning } from '../_shared/researchKinds.ts';
+
+const corsHeaders = {
+  'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
+  'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'authorization, x-client-info, apikey, content-type',
+  'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'POST, OPTIONS',
+};
+
+interface RequestBody {
+  project_id: string;
+  project_slug?: string;
+  owner_id?: string;
+  design_research_id?: string;
+  competitor_url?: string;
+  competitor_index?: number;
+  force?: boolean;
+}
+
+const KIND = 'competitors';
+const COMMAND = '/research-competitors';
+
+serve(
+  withDevLogs(async (req: Request): Promise<Response> => {
+    if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
+      return new Response(null, { headers: corsHeaders });
+    }
+    if (req.method !== 'POST') {
+      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Method not allowed' }), {
+        status: 405,
+        headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
+      });
+    }
+
+    let body: RequestBody;
+    try {
+      body = await req.json();
+    } catch {
+      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Invalid JSON body' }), {
+        status: 400,
+        headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
+      });
+    }
+
+    if (!body.project_id || typeof body.project_id !== 'string') {
+      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'project_id is required' }), {
+        status: 400,
+        headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
+      });
+    }
+
+    let supabase;
+    try {
+      supabase = getServiceClient();
+    } catch (e) {
+      devLog(`${COMMAND}: ${(e as Error).message}`);
+      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Server configuration error' }), {
+        status: 500,
+        headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
+      });
+    }
+
+    if (!body.force) {
+      const current = await currentKindStatus(supabase, body.project_id, KIND);
+      if (current === 'running' || current === 'ready') {
+        devLog(`${COMMAND}: kind '${KIND}' already ${current} — skipping enqueue`);
+        return new Response(
+          JSON.stringify({ status: 'skipped', reason: `kind already ${current}` }),
+          { status: 200, headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' } }
+        );
+      }
+    }
+
+    const resolvedSlug = body.project_slug ?? body.project_id;
+    await markKindRunning(supabase, body.project_id, KIND);
+
+    const { data, error } = await supabase
+      .from('cli_queue')
+      .insert({
+        project_id: body.project_id,
+        command: COMMAND,
+        params: {
+          project_id: body.project_id,
+          project_slug: resolvedSlug,
+          owner_id: body.owner_id ?? null,
+          design_research_id: body.design_research_id ?? null,
+          competitor_url: body.competitor_url ?? null,
+          competitor_index: body.competitor_index ?? null,
+          kind: KIND,
+        },
+        status: 'queued',
+      })
+      .select('id')
+      .single();
+
+    if (error) {
+      devLog(`Failed to enqueue ${COMMAND}: ${error.message}`);
+      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: `Failed to queue command: ${error.message}` }), {
+        status: 500,
+        headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
+      });
+    }
+
+    devLog(`${COMMAND} queued: ${data.id}`);
+    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ command_id: data.id as string, status: 'queued' }), {
+      status: 200,
+      headers: { ...corsHeaders, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
+    });
+  })
+);

+-- Migration 019: Research results — multi-kind research state on design_research.
+--
+-- Phase 4 introduces 5 research kinds: conventions, brand-dna, proof-points,
+-- competitors (re-use), differentiation. Each kind has independent lifecycle
+-- (pending → running → ready/failed) and its own payload blob.
+--
+-- Approach: add a single additive JSONB column `research_kinds` that stores
+-- one entry per kind. Keyed uniqueness (project_id, kind) is enforced by
+-- JSONB key semantics at the application layer via the idempotent
+-- `ensureRequiredResearch` dispatcher. No parallel table is created.
+--
+-- Shape of `research_kinds`:
+--   {
+--     "<kind>": {
+--       "status":     "pending" | "running" | "ready" | "failed",
+--       "payload":    jsonb,            -- kind-specific result payload, nullable
+--       "error":      text | null,
+--       "updated_at": timestamptz (ISO-8601)
+--     }
+--   }
+
+ALTER TABLE design_research
+  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS research_kinds JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb;
+
+COMMENT ON COLUMN design_research.research_kinds IS
+  'Per-kind research state (conventions, brand-dna, proof-points, competitors, differentiation). Shape: { "<kind>": { "status", "payload", "error", "updated_at" } }.';
+
+-- GIN index so the `kind` key lookups (research_kinds ? 'conventions') and
+-- status checks (research_kinds -> 'conventions' ->> 'status' = 'ready')
+-- stay cheap even when many projects are queried in parallel.
+CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_design_research_research_kinds
+  ON design_research USING gin (research_kinds jsonb_path_ops);
